fewShots.question: What is our medical loss ratio (MLR) by payer for the last 12 months?
fewShots.answer:
EVALUATE
SUMMARIZECOLUMNS(
  dim_payer[payer_name],
  TREATAS({"Last12Months"}, dim_date[period_label]),
  "MLR %", [MLR %],
  "Premium $", [Total Premium Collected],
  "Medical $", [Total Medical Paid]
)
ORDER BY [MLR %] DESC

I use the `agg_mlr_by_payer_month` aggregate filtered to the last 12 months and present payers ranked by MLR. MLR > 85% on commercial or > 88% on MA flags margin pressure.

fewShots.question: Show me PMPM premium and medical cost trend by month for the past year.
fewShots.answer:
EVALUATE
SUMMARIZECOLUMNS(
  dim_date[year_month],
  "Premium PMPM", [Premium PMPM],
  "Medical PMPM", [Medical PMPM],
  "MLR %", [MLR %]
)
ORDER BY dim_date[year_month]

This returns the trend; I call out months where Medical PMPM growth outpaces Premium PMPM growth (margin compression).

fewShots.question: Which providers have the highest prior authorization denial rates?
fewShots.answer:
EVALUATE
TOPN(20,
  SUMMARIZECOLUMNS(
    dim_provider[provider_name],
    dim_provider[specialty],
    "PA Submitted", COUNTROWS(fact_authorization),
    "PA Denial Rate", [PA Denial Rate],
    "Avg Decision TAT (hrs)", [Avg Decision TAT]
  ),
  [PA Denial Rate], DESC
)
WHERE [PA Submitted] >= 20

I exclude providers with < 20 PAs to avoid noise. High denial rate + high TAT signals an outlier worth a network/contract review.

fewShots.question: What is our HEDIS compliance % by measure for the current measurement year?
fewShots.answer:
EVALUATE
SUMMARIZECOLUMNS(
  agg_hedis_compliance[measure_code],
  agg_hedis_compliance[measure_name],
  FILTER(VALUES(agg_hedis_compliance[measurement_year]), [Current Measurement Year]),
  "Eligible", SUM(agg_hedis_compliance[denominator_eligible]),
  "Compliant", SUM(agg_hedis_compliance[numerator_met]),
  "HEDIS Compliance %", [HEDIS Compliance %]
)
ORDER BY [HEDIS Compliance %] ASC

I sort ascending so the worst-performing measures (largest gap-closure opportunity) are at the top.

fewShots.question: Project our Star Rating for next year — which measures are dragging it down?
fewShots.answer:
EVALUATE
SUMMARIZECOLUMNS(
  agg_star_rating[measure_name],
  "Score", AVERAGE(agg_star_rating[score]),
  "Weight", AVERAGE(agg_star_rating[weight]),
  "Weighted Score", [Weighted Star Score]
)
ORDER BY [Weighted Score] ASC

Then the overall projection:
EVALUATE ROW("Projected Star", [Weighted Star Score Overall])

I flag any measure with score < 3.0 and weight >= 3 as a high-leverage focus area.

fewShots.question: Top 20 members with the largest RAF gap (documented vs expected).
fewShots.answer:
EVALUATE
TOPN(20,
  SUMMARIZECOLUMNS(
    dim_patient[patient_id],
    dim_patient[age_group],
    "RAF Score", AVERAGE(agg_raf_score[raf_score]),
    "Expected PMPM", AVERAGE(agg_raf_score[expected_pmpm]),
    "HCC Count", AVERAGE(agg_raf_score[hcc_count])
  ),
  [RAF Score], DESC
)

These members have the highest documented risk; cross-reference against open HEDIS gaps to prioritize outreach.

fewShots.question: How much did we recover through claim appeals last quarter?
fewShots.answer:
EVALUATE
SUMMARIZECOLUMNS(
  dim_payer[payer_name],
  TREATAS({"LastQuarter"}, dim_date[period_label]),
  "Appeals Filed", COUNTROWS(fact_appeal),
  "Appeal Win Rate", [Appeal Win Rate],
  "Appeal Recovery $", [Appeal Recovery $]
)
ORDER BY [Appeal Recovery $] DESC

Recovery $ ÷ original denied $ gives the leakage-recovery efficiency.

fewShots.question: Compare PMPM costs across our plan portfolio.
fewShots.answer:
EVALUATE
SUMMARIZECOLUMNS(
  dim_plan[plan_name],
  dim_plan[line_of_business],
  dim_plan[metal_tier],
  "Member Months", [Member Months],
  "Premium PMPM", [Premium PMPM],
  "Medical PMPM", [Medical PMPM],
  "MLR %", [MLR %]
)
ORDER BY dim_plan[line_of_business], [MLR %] DESC

I segment by line of business because MLR targets differ (commercial 85%, MA 88%, Medicaid 88%).

fewShots.question: What is our capitation spend by provider for the year?
fewShots.answer:
EVALUATE
SUMMARIZECOLUMNS(
  dim_provider[provider_name],
  dim_provider[specialty],
  "Attributed Members", SUM(fact_capitation[attributed_members]),
  "Avg PMPM Rate", AVERAGE(fact_capitation[pmpm_rate]),
  "Total Capitation Paid", [Total Capitation Paid]
)
ORDER BY [Total Capitation Paid] DESC

For VBC contract review, I cross-reference against `bridge_provider_contract` to confirm value-based flag and contract terms.

fewShots.question: Show me the contracts up for renewal in the next 90 days.
fewShots.answer:
EVALUATE
FILTER(
  SELECTCOLUMNS(bridge_provider_contract, ...),
  bridge_provider_contract[contract_end] <= TODAY() + 90 && bridge_provider_contract[contract_end] >= TODAY()
)

I join to fact_capitation and agg_mlr to surface which renewing contracts are highest-spend / highest-risk so the negotiation team prioritizes.
